stem: A agricultural research study surveys 20 seeds planted and observes 3 positive outcomes out of 20 (15.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (12 observations, rate = 8.3%) and Group B (8 observations, rate = 25.0%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 31% and 47% of the population. Estimate the true germination rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.15. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.1907 (range [0.1786, 0.2029]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.0524, 0.3604]. Point estimate: 0.1907.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.39: 0.39×0.098 + 0.61×0.25 = 0.1907.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=20, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
